adjudication.label: forming_team
adjudication.rationale: Both individuals converged on the same stealth company (Core Automation) at the exact same start date with explicit co-founder titles, despite very different tenures at OpenAI, strongly indicating intentional team formation rather than coincidence or layoff; the only disconfirming note is the group is small (2 people) and one had only 1 month tenure at OpenAI, suggesting possible opportunistic recruitment rather than a long-gestating plan.

dossier.summary: Two ex-OpenAI operators co-founded stealth startup Core Automation in April 2026. Julia Villagra (former Chief People Officer, OpenAI; prior Head of People, Hudson River Trading) pairs with Francis Z. (32 months at OpenAI, technical staff). Both joined simultaneously, company is tiny/stealth, destination convergence is perfect. No public description yet.
members: Julia Villagra – ex-CPO OpenAI, ex-Head of People Hudson River Trading; Co-Founder/Ops
Francis Z. – 32-month OpenAI technical tenure; Co-Founder/Technical Staff
evidence_timeline: ~Aug 2023: Francis Z. joins OpenAI (implied by 32-month tenure ending ~Apr 2026)
Apr 2026: Both Julia Villagra and Francis Z. simultaneously depart OpenAI and co-found Core Automation
Apr 2026: Core Automation appears in stealth; no public product, no open roles flagged (open_to: 0.0)
dossier.thesis: A senior ops/people executive and a long-tenured technical insider from OpenAI are building something together under the name 'Core Automation' — likely AI-native workflow or enterprise automation, given their combined background in scaling frontier-AI orgs and technical infrastructure. The synchronized exit, stealth posture, and founder-only headcount signal early pre-seed formation. The 32-month OpenAI technical tenure on the engineering side is the real signal; Villagra's CPO pedigree suggests they intend to scale fast and hire aggressively once the product thesis is locked.
dossier.recommended_action: Reach out immediately via warm OpenAI network path. Offer pre-product-market-fit capital or strategic intro before they run a formal process. Anchor conversation on Villagra (more public profile); use her HRT→OpenAI arc as the opener.

adjudication.label: coincidental
adjudication.rationale: Only two people moving to a large destination (Google) with no stealth/founder signal and zero open_to score makes this most likely independent career moves rather than intentional team formation, despite tight timing.

dossier.summary: Two OpenAI alumni — a VP-level engineering leader and a senior AI/ML recruiting specialist — both landed at Google simultaneously in April 2026 after overlapping ~2-year stints at OpenAI. The pairing of a senior engineering exec (Ben Goodger) with a dedicated AI/ML sourcer (Gerald Torres) is structurally significant: it suggests an active, coordinated team-build at Google Labs, not coincidental lateral moves.
members: Ben Goodger — VP Engineering, Labs @ Google (joined Apr 2026); ~22 months at OpenAI
Gerald Torres — L6/L7 Cloud AI/ML SWE Sourcer @ Google (joined Apr 2026); ~24 months at OpenAI
evidence_timeline: ~Jun 2024: Gerald Torres joins OpenAI (inferred from 24-month tenure)
~Jun 2024: Ben Goodger joins OpenAI (inferred from 22-month tenure)
Apr 2026: Both depart OpenAI and surface simultaneously at Google — destination_convergence = 1.0, window_tightness = 1.0
Apr 2026: Goodger titled VP Engineering, Labs — a senior leadership seat implying mandate to staff a team
Apr 2026: Torres titled Cloud AI/ML SWE Sourcer — role directly supports headcount build under Goodger or adjacent Labs leadership
dossier.thesis: Goodger is standing up or expanding a Cloud AI/ML engineering team within Google Labs and brought Torres along specifically to source for it. Their co-tenure at OpenAI means Torres has direct knowledge of Goodger's hiring bar, target profiles, and likely candidate pipeline from OpenAI's talent network. This cluster signals an imminent, high-velocity hiring wave for senior AI/ML engineers into Google Labs — sourced heavily from OpenAI and adjacent frontier-AI orgs. For a talent scout or competing firm, this is a leading indicator of: (1) Google Labs prioritizing cloud-native AI/ML infrastructure, and (2) OpenAI experiencing quiet senior-layer attrition worth monitoring.
dossier.recommended_action: Engage Ben Goodger directly on his team-build scope and open roles; map his OpenAI-era network for candidates he and Torres will target. Simultaneously, audit OpenAI's L6/L7 engineering bench for secondary departure signals. If representing candidates, fast-track any cloud AI/ML SWEs with OpenAI or DeepMind backgrounds — Torres is actively sourcing this profile now.

adjudication.label: coincidental
adjudication.rationale: All five landed at Anthropic in April 2026 with tight timing, but they span unrelated functions (policy, engineering, GTM, research) with low co-tenure overlap (0.3), no stealth/founding signal, and Anthropic is a large well-known rival that routinely recruits from OpenAI en masse, making independent individual hires the more parsimonious explanation than coordinated team formation.

dossier.summary: Five ex-OpenAI employees converged on Anthropic in a tight April 2026 wave. Roles span technical staff (3x MTS), product policy/frontier risk, and GTM strategy — a cross-functional cluster suggesting coordinated or socially-networked departure, not random attrition. Prior OpenAI tenures range 11–26 months, indicating mid-to-senior contributors, not entry-level churn.
members: Bilva C. — Product Policy, Frontier Risk @ Anthropic (Apr 2026); 12mo OpenAI tenure
Abby C. — MTS @ Anthropic (Apr 2026); 14.9mo OpenAI tenure; self-flagged 'Ex-OpenAI'
Katherine Hill — MTS @ Anthropic (Apr 2026); 11mo OpenAI tenure
Tim Salimans — MTS @ Anthropic (Apr 2026); 26mo OpenAI tenure — longest tenure, likely senior signal
Yan Pu — GTM Strategy & Ops @ Anthropic (Apr 2026); 13mo OpenAI tenure
evidence_timeline: ~May 2024–Jun 2025: Members join OpenAI across overlapping windows (inferred from tenure lengths)
Apr 2026: All 5 start at Anthropic simultaneously — window_tightness score = 1.0 (maximum)
Apr 2026: destination_convergence = 1.0, shared_destination = 1.0 — zero dispersion to other companies
co_tenure = 0.3 — partial but meaningful overlap at OpenAI, not all in same team/time
dossier.thesis: This is a high-conviction coordinated talent exodus from OpenAI to Anthropic. The April 2026 synchronization across 5 people in distinct functions (safety policy, engineering, GTM) signals a network-pull event — likely one anchor hire triggered referrals. Tim Salimans (26mo tenure) is the probable anchor given seniority. Cross-functional composition (not just engineers) suggests Anthropic may be building out a specific product or safety initiative requiring a full stack team. This cluster is a strong leading indicator of OpenAI organizational stress or a targeted Anthropic recruiting push in safety/frontier-risk domains.
dossier.recommended_action: Reach out to Tim Salimans and Bilva C. immediately — Salimans as likely anchor/connector, Bilva as rare policy+frontier-risk profile. Map their OpenAI networks for additional departure signals. For investors: flag Anthropic's ability to recruit cross-functional OpenAI talent at scale as a competitive moat signal worth weighting in diligence.

adjudication.label: coincidental
adjudication.rationale: Only two people converging on the same large employer (Google DeepMind) with zero co-tenure overlap and zero stealth/founder signal is more consistent with independent talent poaching by a major competitor than deliberate team formation.
